skill.name: architect
skill.description: Plan system architecture, compare implementation options, and document high-level technical decisions for complex software work.
skill.summary: Create structured architecture plans with tradeoffs, risks, dependencies, and rollout guidance.
skill.author: 0xSero

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by keyword matches inside ordinary words such as description and design. No cryptographic implementation, executable code, network access, filesystem access, or prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Keyword Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md lines 3, 12, and 21. These locations contain descriptive text and YAML labels using words such as description and design, not cryptographic code or unsafe algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is non-code prose and metadata, and no cryptographic functions or algorithm names are used as implementations. The repeated line 3 alerts appear to be substring matches rather than separate risks.

content.value_statement: Complex features often fail when teams skip requirements, tradeoffs, and rollout planning. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce structured architecture plans before implementation starts.
actual_capabilities: Clarifies requirements, constraints, dependencies, and open decisions before proposing a design.
Compares architecture alternatives with practical pros, cons, and risk notes.
Creates structured implementation plans for complex features or system changes.
Identifies technical risks, stakeholder alignment needs, and phased rollout options.
Reviews high-level architecture decisions without modifying files or running commands.
limitations: Does not edit code, write files, or run shell commands because tool access is disabled.
Does not verify architecture assumptions against live systems unless context is provided.
Does not replace security, compliance, or production readiness reviews by specialists.
Depends on accurate requirements, constraints, and repository context from the user.

use_cases.title: Plan a Complex Feature
use_cases.description: Break a large feature into architecture choices, dependencies, milestones, and rollout steps.

use_cases.title: Compare Technical Options
use_cases.description: Evaluate competing designs with tradeoffs, risks, implementation cost, and decision criteria.

use_cases.title: Prepare an Architecture Review
use_cases.description: Create a concise overview of system design, open questions, and stakeholder decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Architecture Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan the architecture for this feature: [feature]. Include requirements, constraints, main components, risks, and rollout steps.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Designs
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these architecture options: [option A] and [option B]. Explain tradeoffs, risks, dependencies, and a recommended path.

prompt_templates.title: Review an Existing Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this architecture proposal: [proposal]. Find unclear assumptions, missing dependencies, operational risks, and decisions needing alignment.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Phased Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a phased migration from [current system] to [target system]. Include compatibility strategy, risk controls, rollback options, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Plan a new notification service for product events.
output_examples.output: A structured plan covering event sources, delivery channels, user preferences, reliability concerns, observability, rollout phases, and unresolved decisions.

output_examples.input: Compare synchronous API calls with an event-driven workflow.
output_examples.output: A tradeoff review covering latency, coupling, failure handling, operational complexity, migration cost, and a recommended decision path.

output_examples.input: Review a proposal to split a monolith module into a service.
output_examples.output: A review identifying service boundaries, data ownership questions, deployment risks, monitoring needs, and stakeholder decisions.

best_practices: Provide business goals, nonfunctional requirements, current constraints, and known dependencies.
Ask for explicit tradeoffs when multiple designs are possible.
Use the output as a review artifact before committing to implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for final architecture decisions without sharing constraints and success criteria.
Do not treat the plan as verified production guidance without engineering review.
Do not use this skill for direct code changes or command execution.

faq.question: Can this skill change files or run commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill configuration disables write, edit, and bash tools.

faq.question: What type of work is this skill best for?
faq.answer: It is best for system design, feature planning, architecture reviews, and technical decision support.

faq.question: Does it create implementation code?
faq.answer: No. It creates plans, tradeoff analysis, review notes, and rollout guidance.

faq.question: Can it help with migration planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline phased migrations, compatibility concerns, rollback options, and validation steps.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide goals, constraints, existing architecture, dependencies, deadlines, and known risks.

faq.question: Is this suitable for security architecture review?
faq.answer: It can identify design-level risks, but specialist security review is still needed for high-risk systems.
